title: Dealing with Police
description: Covers various aspects of dealing with police including safety, questioning, searches, infiltrators and informants, and documenting interactions.

title: Basic Legal Info
description: Half-sheet condensed version of Dealing with Police covering essential know-your-rights basics.

title: Legal Steps and Choices Chart
description: Flowchart showing typical steps through the criminal legal system from arrest to trial and choices facing defendants at each step.

title: Police Misconduct Report
description: Form designed to help victims of police misconduct recall important details and record them in a useful format.

title: Legal Support for a Small Action
description: Handout for the Small Action Legal Support training, oriented to actions with fewer than 50 arrests.

title: Setting Up an Action Legal Team
description: Helps organizers form an effective legal team for a mass action, covering questions, lawyer relations, and legal research.

title: Legal Team Action Roles
description: Describes the various roles that need to be filled during an action, including office and street positions.

title: Office Setup
description: Covers aspects of setting up an action legal office, including physical setup and materials needed.

title: Phone Script
description: Guide for answering phones at an action legal office for jail, street, media, and family calls.

title: Arrestee Intake Form
description: Form to collect information from arrestees for the legal office file.

title: Arrestee Outtake Form
description: Form to collect information from arrestees when they are released from jail.

title: Organizing Mass Defense
description: Overview of legal support work for taking a large number of political cases to trial.

title: Legal Support Person
description: Covers basic information about how to be the legal support person for your affinity group.

title: Legal Solidarity Handbook
description: Covers the basics of Legal Solidarity in jail and court, including police, media, plea bargains, jail, court, and lawyers.

title: Court Solidarity
description: In-depth guide to using Legal Solidarity in court, with discussion of court support, lawyers, and plea bargains.

title: Know Your Rights Sample Training Agenda and Guide
description: Agenda for a typical Midnight Special Know Your Rights training, including role plays, raps, sample confession, and role play cards.

title: Know Your Rights Trainer's Guide
description: Contains all the dim-sum role plays trainers can mix and match according to individual needs.

title: Know Your Rights Trainer Training
description: Training-for-trainers resource with pointers on how to act like a cop and deliver the KYR raps.

title: Know Your Rights Trainer Training Handout
description: Handout with examples of role plays, expanded sample dialog, and tips on how to act like a cop.

title: Legal Observer Trainer's Guide
description: Prepares Legal Observers for mass actions, focused on collecting information useful for criminal defense and civil prosecution.

title: Legal Observer Trainer Training
description: Teaches trainers how to deliver the Legal Observer training, with focus on teaching methods.

title: Small Action Legal Support Trainer's Guide
description: Prepares participants to provide legal support for a small action (1-30 arrests), suitable for affinity groups.

title: Computer Security Trainer's Guide
description: Two-hour training teaching computer users how to protect their data and communications via good security habits.

title: Non-Violent Street Tactics Trainer's Guide
description: Agenda and curriculum for the Non-Violent Direct Action training.

title: Grand Jury Trainer's Guide
description: One-hour training covering how to protect yourself and your community if called before a federal Grand Jury.

title: Legal Solidarity Trainer's Guide
description: Four-hour training on using collective action in the streets and courts.

title: Legal Observer/Video Observer Guide
description: Comprehensive guide for activists, law students, or lawyers to learn the basics of legal observing including note-taking, preparation, and equipment.

title: Computer Security Handout
description: Covers secure passwords, data creation and destruction, and email security for activists.

title: Shooting the Wounded - Activist Version
description: Guide for activists on documenting injuries for use in lawsuits, media, and criminal defense.

title: Shooting the Wounded - Non-Activist Version
description: Non-activist version of the injury-documentation guide for lawsuits, media, and criminal defense.

title: Grand Jury FAQ
description: Frequently asked questions about federal grand juries and how to resist them.

title: Grand Jury Backgrounder
description: Background primer on how grand juries function and their use against political movements.

title: Grand Jury Fact Sheet
description: Concise fact sheet on grand jury basics and resistance.
